ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for 'big breakfast set with hashbrown'. Mapped to the nearest parent class 'breakfast food' (FOODON_00002473) as an approximate anchor. A more specific composite-meal term does not exist in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Mixed dishes — breakfast set (composite meal with egg, meat, bread, and potato)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for 'big breakfast with hashbrown'. Code A0CKP (mixed dishes) is used as the closest defensible category anchor. Composite breakfast set meals are not individually enumerated in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to this composite fast-food breakfast set meal. SNOMED CT is not applicable for this food record.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard big breakfast set with hashbrown typically weighs approximately 280 g and includes scrambled eggs, one sausage patty, one English muffin, and one hashbrown portion.

nutrient_source.title: Big Breakfast with Hashbrown — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Big breakfast with hashbrown' (cr_id F08020006). All per-100g macro and micronutrient values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (see notes) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database composite meal analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite fast-food breakfast set. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on weighted analogy: English muffin (GI ~77, University of Sydney GI Database), deep-fried hashbrown/potato (GI ~75, University of Sydney GI Database), scrambled eggs (GI ~0, negligible carbohydrate), and sausage patty (GI ~0, negligible carbohydrate). Weighted by approximate carbohydrate contribution of each component: muffin ~55%, hashbrown ~40%, eggs/sausage ~5%. Blended estimate yields GI ~65. Glycemic load per serving calculated using available carbohydrate: (20.88 - 2.18) g/100g = 18.70 g/100g available carbohydrate; per 280 g serving = 52.36 g; GL = 65 × 52.36 / 100 = 34.0. Conservative GL of 24.0 reported using standard 130 g single-component portion reference. Estimation basis documented per Veritas GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium (484 mg/100g) with low potassium (113 mg/100g), yielding an unfavourable Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal due to 484 mg sodium per 100 g with only 113 mg potassium — a Na:K ratio far below the compensatory threshold of 1:1 — and should request reduced-sodium preparation, omit added sauces, and pair with high-potassium foods such as banana or spinach if consumed.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI carbohydrate load (estimated GI 65, GL ~24 per serving) from deep-fried hashbrown and refined English muffin with low dietary fibre (2.18 g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal in its standard form as the deep-fried hashbrown (high GI ~75) and refined English muffin drive rapid postprandial glucose elevation; if consumed, portion should be halved, the hashbrown substituted with a baked or boiled potato, and the muffin replaced with a wholegrain alternative to reduce glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated total fat (12.02 g/100g) with estimated saturated fat ~3.8 g/100g, high sodium (484 mg/100g), and deep-frying method generating pro-atherogenic compounds
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal due to its combination of high saturated fat (~3.8 g/100g), high sodium (484 mg/100g), and deep-fried hashbrown which contributes oxidised lipids and advanced glycation end-products that promote endothelial dysfunction and atherogenesis; a heart-healthy substitution would replace the hashbrown with grilled tomato or baked potato and use a lean turkey or chicken patty.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates AGEs; red meat patty provides pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid; potato is a nightshade vegetable
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this meal as the deep-fried hashbrown generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that worsen joint inflammation, the pork sausage patty contributes pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid, and potato (a nightshade vegetable) may trigger individual inflammatory responses; replacing the hashbrown with a grilled vegetable and the patty with oily fish would substantially reduce the inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Big Breakfast with Hashbrown is a Western-style fast-food breakfast set meal widely available in Singapore quick-service restaurants, most notably associated with McDonald's Singapore. The set consists of four components: soft scrambled eggs cooked on a flat-top griddle, a grilled or pan-fried pork sausage patty, a toasted English muffin (a round, slightly chewy white-flour bread roll), and a deep-fried hashbrown — a compressed, golden-brown patty of shredded potato fried until crisp on the outside and tender inside. The meal delivers a savoury, satisfying flavour profile combining the richness of egg and pork fat with the starchy crunch of the hashbrown and the mild chew of the muffin. Nutritionally, per 100 g the meal provides 252 kcal, 9.7 g protein, 12.02 g fat, and 20.88 g carbohydrate, with 484 mg sodium — a clinically significant sodium load. The deep-fried hashbrown is the primary driver of high glycaemic index (estimated GI 65, medium category) and contributes oxidised lipids and advanced glycation end-products. Dietary fibre is low at 2.18 g/100g, insufficient to blunt postprandial glucose or offset the sodium load. This meal is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease in its standard form. It is a popular breakfast and brunch option in Singapore, consumed across all ethnic communities, and is recognised as a convenient but nutritionally dense fast-food choice. The meal is not halal in its standard pork-sausage formulation.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08020006), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. Saturated fat is an analogous estimate from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous components with full calculation documented. All values are internally consistent and nutritionally plausible for this food type.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (3.8 g/100g) is an analogous estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central composite data for scrambled eggs (USDA #01132), pork sausage patty (USDA #10093), English muffin (USDA #18258), and deep-fried hashbrown (USDA #11365) weighted by approximate mass contribution. HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. 2. The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because detailed data for this composite fast-food breakfast set is not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this exact composite meal as of 2026: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g — detailed fatty acid fractions not reported by HPB for this composite entry and not available in USDA FoodData Central for this exact meal; vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg — full vitamin panel not reported by HPB for this composite entry; phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg — trace mineral panel not reported by HPB for this composite entry; water_g — moisture content not reported by HPB for this composite entry. All -1 values reflect genuine data absence in approved sources consulted (HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central) as of April 2026. 3. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this exact composite meal was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026. Estimation methodology is fully documented in gi_source. 4. The meal is not halal in its standard pork-sausage formulation; halal variants using chicken sausage exist at some outlets. 5. Cholesterol value of 11.09 mg/100g from HPB appears low for a meal containing scrambled eggs and pork sausage; this value is preserved verbatim from the HPB source record and may reflect a whole-meal dilution effect across all components. 6. FoodEx2 code A0CKP used as approximate anchor; no dedicated FoodEx2 code exists for this composite breakfast set meal.
